Region.title: Region
Region.description: Represents a geographical region (country, state, province) for which epidemiological data is collected and predictions are made.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Region entity.

name.description: The common name of the region (e.g., 'United States', 'California').

isoCode.description: Standard ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or similar code for the region (e.g., 'US', 'US-CA').

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate for the central point of the region, used for map visualization.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate for the central point of the region, used for map visualization.

EpidemiologicalRecord.title: EpidemiologicalRecord
EpidemiologicalRecord.description: Stores daily aggregated epidemiological and related data for a specific region, integrating information from various sources like Johns Hopkins, Our World in Data, and Google Mobility.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EpidemiologicalRecord entity.

regionId.description: Reference to the Region this record belongs to. (Relationship: Region 1:N EpidemiologicalRecord)

recordDate.description: The specific date for which this epidemiological data is recorded.

totalCases.description: The cumulative total number of confirmed cases up to this date.

newCases.description: The number of new confirmed cases reported on this date.

totalDeaths.description: The cumulative total number of deaths up to this date.

newDeaths.description: The number of new deaths reported on this date.

totalRecovered.description: The cumulative total number of recovered individuals up to this date.

newRecovered.description: The number of new recovered individuals reported on this date.

totalVaccinations.description: The cumulative total number of vaccine doses administered up to this date (optional, from Our World in Data).

peopleVaccinated.description: The cumulative total number of people who have received at least one vaccine dose up to this date (optional, from Our World in Data).

peopleFullyVaccinated.description: The cumulative total number of people who are fully vaccinated up to this date (optional, from Our World in Data).

mobilityRetailRecreationChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for retail and recreation places compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

mobilityGroceryPharmacyChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for grocery and pharmacy places compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

mobilityParksChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for parks compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

mobilityTransitStationsChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for transit stations compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

mobilityWorkplacesChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for workplaces compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

mobilityResidentialChange.description: Percentage change in mobility for residential areas compared to a baseline (optional, from Google Mobility).

Prediction.title: Prediction
Prediction.description: Stores the results of an AI-powered epidemic prediction for a specific region and future date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prediction entity.

regionId.description: Reference to the Region for which this prediction was made. (Relationship: Region 1:N Prediction)

predictionDate.description: The future date for which the case count is predicted.

predictedCases.description: The forecasted number of active cases for the prediction date.

riskLevel.description: The calculated risk level for the region on the prediction date (Low, Medium, High).

predictionTimestamp.description: The timestamp when this prediction was generated.

modelVersion.description: Identifier for the AI/ML model version used to generate this prediction (e.g., 'LSTM-v1.2').

Insight.title: Insight
Insight.description: Stores AI-generated analytical insights regarding epidemiological trends, factors, and anomalies.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Insight entity.

regionId.description: Reference to the Region this insight is about. Can be null for global insights. (Relationship: Region 0..1:N Insight)

insightDate.description: The date when this insight was generated or is most relevant.

type.description: The category or type of insight (e.g., 'Mobility Impact', 'Vaccination Effect', 'Trend Analysis', 'Anomaly Detection').

summary.description: A brief summary of the insight.

details.description: The full, detailed content of the insight.

relatedPredictionId.description: Optional reference to a specific Prediction entity if this insight is directly related to it. (Relationship: Prediction 0..1:N Insight)

definition.description: Stores metadata for geographical regions (countries, states). This collection is publicly readable for dashboard display and administrator writable for data management.

Contains daily epidemiological and related data for a specific region. This subcollection is publicly readable and administrator writable. The document's 'regionId' field must match the path's '{regionId}' for data integrity.

params.name: regionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Region.

params.name: recordId
params.description: The unique identifier for the epidemiological record.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated epidemic predictions for a specific region and future date. This subcollection is publicly readable and administrator writable. The document's 'regionId' field must match the path's '{regionId}' for data integrity.

params.name: regionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Region.

params.name: predictionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the prediction.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated analytical insights, which can be global or associated with a specific region (via an optional 'regionId' field). This collection is publicly readable and administrator writable.

params.name: insightId
params.description: The unique identifier for the insight.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document with a user's UID here grants them administrative privileges for write operations across the application data. This collection is typically managed by a super-admin or service account, with very restricted read/write access.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'EpiGuard AI' prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access based on a public read / admin write model. 

**Authorization Independence:** Given that the core data (Regions, EpidemiologicalRecords, Predictions, Insights) is designed for public read access, the authorization context for reading is global. Therefore, there's no need to denormalize parent-specific access control attributes (like `ownerId` or `members` maps) into subcollection documents to determine read permissions. All users, authenticated or unauthenticated, can read this data. Write access is controlled by a top-level administrative role system (`/roles_admin/{adminUid}`). This completely decouples write authorization from any data hierarchy, ensuring write operations are atomic and authorization rules are simple (e.g., `allow write: exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`). The `regionId` field within subcollection documents (`epidemiologicalRecords`, `predictions`) is primarily for data integrity and logical grouping, ensuring the document's `regionId` matches its path segment, rather than for authorization purposes.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Predictors):** For read operations, since all primary data collections are publicly readable, `list` queries are inherently secure. Any user can perform `list` operations on `/regions`, `/regions/{regionId}/epidemiologicalRecords`, `/regions/{regionId}/predictions`, and `/insights` without complex filtering by security rules. The rules simply grant `read` access universally. This enables efficient querying for the dashboard UI without requiring complex, potentially inefficient rule-based filtering. For write operations, the presence of `request.auth.uid` in the `/roles_admin` collection acts as the authorization predictor, ensuring only designated administrators can modify data.

**Structural Segregation:** Collections like `/regions` house only region entities, and `/insights` houses insights. Subcollections like `/regions/{regionId}/epidemiologicalRecords` and `/regions/{regionId}/predictions` ensure that all documents within them share the exact same security posture (public read / admin write), simplifying rule definitions and preventing mixed-access data within a single collection. Global insights are distinguished from regional ones by an optional `regionId` field in the `/insights` collection, rather than creating separate subcollections for global insights, striking a balance between segregation and queryability.

**Access Modeling & Consistency:**
*   **Global Entities:** `regions` and `insights` are top-level collections for global entities or entities that can be global (like insights).
*   **Hierarchical & Related Data:** `epidemiologicalRecords` and `predictions` are structured as subcollections under `regions` to logically group data and optimize queries for region-specific information (e.g., getting all historical records for a given region).
*   **DBAC:** Admin roles are managed via a dedicated top-level collection (`/roles_admin`), enabling simple existence checks in security rules to grant administrative privileges.

This design ensures clarity, maintainability, and robust security for 'EpiGuard AI'.
